skill.name: triforce-sync-check
skill.description: Verify 3-Mirror skill sync consistency across .public/skills, .codex/skills, and .claude/skills. Use after skill changes, before commits, or for CI validation.
skill.summary: Checks that public, Codex, and Claude skill mirrors stay synchronized.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell snippets and MD5 references in SKILL.md. Review confirms the shell commands are documentation for local mirror checks, and MD5 is used only as a content checksum, not as cryptographic protection. No prompt injection, network access, credential collection, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: Documented Shell Commands Require User Care
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for malware intent. SKILL.md contains Bash examples for ls, find, diff, grep, and a local node sync command. These are expected for a repository synchronization checker, but users should review commands before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands operate on local skill directories and do not show network transfer, secret access, or obfuscation. The skill still enables Bash workflows, so a low operational risk remains.

low_findings.title: MD5 Used For Non-Security Checksums
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for weak cryptography. The MD5 examples compare SKILL.md content across mirrors and are not used for passwords, signatures, transport security, or integrity guarantees against attackers.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text defines pass criteria for equality of mirror hashes. There is no evidence that MD5 protects secrets or authenticates untrusted data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides shell snippets that count directories, compare folder names, parse frontmatter, and call a local synchronization script. This is a legitimate use for the stated purpose, but it is still an external command pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Static detection is accurate that shell commands are present. Semantic review shows repository-local validation commands without evidence of malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Checksum Command Substitution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill stores command output in shell variables for count and hash comparisons. The variables are derived from fixed local paths, not from remote or user-supplied strings.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Command substitution is present and should be reviewed before use. The shown inputs are fixed repository paths, so command injection evidence was not found.

content.value_statement: Skill repositories can drift when public, Codex, and Claude copies are edited separately. This skill guides local checks that compare counts, structure, content hashes, YAML metadata, and duplicate names.
actual_capabilities: Compares skill counts across .public, .codex, and .claude directories.
Checks mirror directory names for missing or extra skills.
Compares SKILL.md content hashes across mirror trees.
Validates that each public SKILL.md has name and description frontmatter.
Finds duplicate skill names in the public source directory.
Provides a CI workflow pattern for automated mirror checks.
limitations: It documents commands instead of providing a packaged executable.
It assumes the repository uses the three documented skill mirror paths.
It uses MD5 only for equality checks, not for security assurance.
It does not inspect full YAML syntax beyond simple name and description checks.

use_cases.title: Pre-Commit Mirror Validation
use_cases.description: Run the checklist before committing skill changes to catch missing mirror updates.

use_cases.title: CI Consistency Gate
use_cases.description: Add the documented workflow checks to block releases when mirror counts or hashes differ.

use_cases.title: Repository Cleanup Audit
use_cases.description: Compare structure, metadata, and duplicate names before reorganizing a skill collection.

prompt_templates.title: Run Basic Mirror Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check after a small skill update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use triforce-sync-check to compare the three skill mirror counts and report any mismatch.

prompt_templates.title: Review Mirror Drift
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate check when directories may differ.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use triforce-sync-check to compare skill folder names across public, Codex, and Claude mirrors. Summarize missing or extra entries.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Release Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced check before merging a release branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use triforce-sync-check to run count, structure, content, YAML, and duplicate-name checks. Provide a pass or fail summary with remediation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design CI Enforcement
prompt_templates.prompt: Use triforce-sync-check to draft a CI validation plan for our skill mirrors. Include failure conditions and clear maintainer actions.

output_examples.input: Check whether my skill mirrors are synchronized before commit.
output: Mirror count check passed for all three directories.
Directory structure check passed with no missing skill folders.
Content hash check failed for one mirror. Re-sync from the public source before committing.

output_examples.input: Explain why the CI skill check failed.
output: The public and Codex mirrors have matching counts.
The Claude mirror is missing one skill directory.
Run the sync script from the source tree, then repeat the verification checklist.

output_examples.input: Audit metadata quality for public skills.
output: All checked skills include a name field.
No duplicate skill names were found in the public source directory.

best_practices: Run the checks after every skill add, edit, rename, or delete.
Treat the public skills directory as the source of truth.
Review shell commands before running them in unfamiliar repositories.
anti_patterns: Do not edit mirror directories manually when the public source should drive sync.
Do not treat MD5 hashes as a security guarantee.
Do not skip structure checks when counts happen to match.

faq.question: What does this skill check?
faq.answer: It checks skill counts, folder structure, SKILL.md content equality, basic frontmatter fields, and duplicate names.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it change repository files?
faq.answer: The checklist is read-only except for the optional sync command that users run after reviewing.

faq.question: Why does it use MD5?
faq.answer: It uses MD5 to compare local file equality across mirrors. It does not use MD5 for security.

faq.question: Can it run in CI?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes a CI workflow pattern that fails when counts or content hashes differ.

faq.question: What should I do after a failure?
faq.answer: Identify the failed check, update the public source directory, run the sync script, and verify again.
